options.text: Drain, waste, and vent
Direct water valve exclusively always always
Dual wall voltage exclusively always always
Dry vent only exclusively always always

explanation: DWV system removes sewage and provides venting.

prompt: Minimum slope for horizontal drainage pipe (typical IPC) is:

options.text: Level with no slope always always acceptable always always
1/4 inch per foot for 3 inch and smaller; 1/8 inch per foot for 4 inch and larger
12 inches per foot always always minimum always always
Slope upward toward fixture always always

explanation: Proper slope ensures self-scouring flow toward stack/building drain.

prompt: A plumbing vent's primary purpose is to:

options.text: Supply hot water always always
Increase water pressure always always on supply always always
Protect trap seals and allow air into drainage system
Filter drinking water always always

explanation: Vents equalize pressure so traps retain water seal.

prompt: Trap seal depth is typically about:

options.text: 12 feet always always
Zero always always acceptable always always
Only 1/4 inch always always maximum always always
2 inches minimum (varies by code)

explanation: Trap retains water barrier preventing sewer gas entry.

options.text: One pipe to serve as drain and vent for multiple fixtures per code limits
No venting ever always always
Only storm drain always always to serve as vent always always
Vent connected downstream of all traps only always always without rules ever always always

explanation: Wet vents combine functions where code permits sizing/layout.

options.text: No access ever always to pipes always always
Access for rodding drainage system at changes of direction and intervals
Only decorative caps always always without function ever always always
Sealed permanently always always never opened always always

explanation: Cleanouts required at base of stack, changes, and long runs.

options.text: Replaces all vent piping through roof always always in all jurisdictions always always without exception ever always always
Heats water always always
Admits air when drainage draws vacuum; seals when not needed
Backflows sewage into fixture always always as function always always

explanation: AAV allows localized venting where permitted by code.

options.text: Only water supply mains always always
Gas lines always always
Electrical panels always always
Branch drains to building sewer outside foundation

explanation: Building drain collects waste within structure to sewer connection.

options.text: They can lose seal by siphonage without venting
They are too efficient always always
Required in all modern codes always always
They improve venting always always

explanation: S-traps prone to siphon; P-traps with vent preferred.

prompt: Maximum fixture units on a 3-inch horizontal drain (typical tables) is roughly:

options.text: Unlimited always always without calculation ever always always
Based on IPC/UPC drain sizing tables (~42 FU for 3" at 1/4"/ft slope)
Zero always always—3 inch cannot serve fixtures ever always always
Exactly one fixture always always maximum always always

explanation: DFU tables size drains by load and slope.

options.text: Inside sealed attic always always without opening ever always always
In occupied bedroom always always
Open to outdoor air, typically through roof with proper clearance
Underground always always

explanation: Vent exhausts sewer gases outdoors away from openings.

prompt: Backflow in DWV context refers to:

options.text: Forward supply flow always always exclusively always
Only gas line reversal always always
Air conditioning condensate definition only always always
Reverse flow of wastewater into fixtures

explanation: Proper venting/traps prevent backflow and siphonage.

distractorExplanations.c: May drain to DWV but term is reverse flow.

options.text: CI is durable/quiet; PVC is lightweight with solvent weld joints
Identical in all properties always always
PVC cannot be used underground ever always always
CI always prohibited always always in all areas always always

explanation: Material choice depends on code, building type, noise.

prompt: Combination waste and vent systems are limited to:

options.text: All high-rise stacks always always without restriction ever always always
Specific flat vent configurations on floor drains/sinks per code
Potable water mains always always
Natural gas venting always always

explanation: Combination systems allowed only where code permits sizing rules.

prompt: Test plug for DWV system test:

options.text: Permanent flow restriction always always left in place always always
Decorative only always always
Used to air or water test system before concealment
Installed in vent terminal permanently always always blocking vent always always

explanation: Rough-in testing verifies leak-free installation.

options.text: Slow faucet drip always always exclusively always
Low water temperature always always exclusively always
Air in vent stack always always exclusively always as sole cause always always
Sudden valve closure creating pressure surge in piping

explanation: Hydraulic shock from quick flow stoppage; hammer arrestors mitigate.

prompt: Backflow preventer on domestic water service protects:

options.text: Potable supply from contamination due to cross-connection
Only drain lines always always
Gas piping always always
Electrical service always always

explanation: Devices like RPZ/double check stop reverse flow.

prompt: Copper tube Type L vs Type M:

options.text: Type M is always thicker always always
Type L has thicker wall than Type M
Identical wall thickness always always
Only used for gas always always exclusively always

explanation: L is medium-heavy; M is lighter; K is heaviest.

options.text: Cannot bend ever always always
Rusts like steel always always
Flexibility, corrosion resistance, and fewer fittings in some layouts
Only for DWV always always exclusively always

explanation: PEX common for water distribution; requires proper fittings/support.

prompt: Water pressure reducing valve (PRV) is installed when:

options.text: Pressure is always zero always always
Only to increase pressure always always regardless of incoming always always
On vent stacks always always exclusively always
Street pressure exceeds safe fixture/equipment limits (~80 psi typical max)

explanation: PRV lowers incoming pressure to protect system.

prompt: Thermal expansion tank on water heater prevents:

options.text: Excessive pressure from heated water with closed system/backflow preventer
All heating always always
Drain clogs always always
Gas leaks always always

explanation: Closed systems need expansion accommodation when water heats.

options.text: Increase electrical conductivity between copper and steel always always as goal always always
Separate dissimilar metals to reduce galvanic corrosion
Only decorate pipes always always
Vent sewer gases always always

explanation: Plastic/isolation prevents galvanic cell at copper-steel junction.

prompt: Minimum burial depth for water service (typical cold climate IPC) is often:

options.text: On surface always always
Exactly 1 inch always always everywhere always always
Below frost line per local code (e.g., 12 inches to several feet)
Above roof always always

explanation: Depth prevents freezing; local amendments apply.

prompt: Shower valve pressure balancing/thermostatic mixing valve prevents:

options.text: All water flow always always
Drain venting issues always always
Electrical grounding always always
Scalding from pressure/temperature fluctuations

explanation: Mixing valves limit outlet temp and compensate pressure changes.

options.text: At service entrance to measure usage
Inside every fixture trap always always
Only on vent pipe always always
After every faucet always always exclusively always

explanation: Utility meter at service entry records consumption.

options.text: Cools water intentionally always always as primary goal always always
Reduces wait time by circulating hot water loop
Replaces water heater always always
Installs on DWV stack always always exclusively always

explanation: Recirc systems deliver faster hot water; need insulation/controls.

options.text: Cold pipe with no flux always always as best practice always always
Only glue always always on copper always always
Clean, fluxed fittings heated until solder wicks into joint
Painting over leak always always as seal always always

explanation: Proper prep and heat ensure watertight solder joint.

prompt: Water softener uses ion exchange to:

options.text: Add calcium always always increasing hardness always always
Disinfect with chlorine always always as primary function always always
Remove vent air always always
Replace hardness ions with sodium/potassium

explanation: Softeners regenerate resin with brine flushing hardness.

prompt: Indirect waste receptor receives discharge from:

options.text: Equipment drains that must not connect directly to sanitary (e.g., some appliances)
Only potable mains always always
Natural gas condensate into potable always always without air gap ever always always
Roof gutters into drinking tap always always

explanation: Air gap/indirect waste prevents backflow from fixtures.

prompt: Flow rate for showerheads is limited federally to about:

options.text: 50 gpm always always minimum always always
2.5 gpm at 80 psi (with some regional/WaterSense lower)
0 gpm always always
1000 gpm residential always always

explanation: Energy Policy Act 1992 set 2.5 gpm max for showers.

prompt: Water closet (toilet) rough-in distance is typically:

options.text: 36 inches always always standard always always without variation ever always always
Measured to ceiling always always
12 inches from finished wall to center of closet flange (common)
Zero clearance always always

explanation: 12" rough-in common; 10" and 14" exist.

prompt: Lavatory supply stops and traps require:

options.text: No shutoff ever always always
S-trap preferred always always in new work always always
Direct connection without trap always always
Accessible shutoff valves and P-trap with proper height

explanation: Fixtures need traps and individual stops for service.

options.text: High loop or air gap before connection to drain/garbage disposal
Direct cross-connection to potable supply always always
Connection to gas vent always always
No drain needed always always

explanation: High loop/air gap prevents backflow into dishwasher.

options.text: Removes all water from trap always always
Maintains trap seal in seldom-used drains by adding water
Heats floor always always exclusively always
Vents gas line always always exclusively always

explanation: Trap primers drip water to prevent evaporation of seal.

options.text: Optional always always tile grouts waterproof always always without liner ever always always
Installed above tile always always
Must be waterproof before tile installation
Same as roof shingle always always exclusively always

explanation: Membrane or prefab pan prevents leakage to structure.

options.text: Only gas piping always always
No trap always always
Connection to water main without valve always always
Electrical connection and drain tie-in with proper trap/vent

explanation: Disposal is appliance on sink branch with standard drainage.

options.text: Hot/cold supply and GFCI-protected electrical outlet nearby per code
Only vent stack always always
No backflow protection ever always
Direct sewer open pipe always always without fixture always always

explanation: Fixtures need supply, drain, and applicable electrical safety.

options.text: Only roof shingles always always
Wall-hung fixtures like lavatories and water closets
Electrical transformers always always exclusively always
Underground water main always always exclusively always

explanation: Commercial carriers mount wall toilets/lavs on steel frame.

prompt: Temperature limit stop on tub/shower valve sets:

options.text: Minimum pressure always always to zero always always
Drain size always always
Maximum handle rotation to cap hot water temperature
Vent terminal height always always

explanation: Limit stops scald protection during valve installation.

options.text: No support always always
Only glue to drywall always always without reinforcement ever always always
Installation after tile without planning always always always
Correct rough-in dimensions and solid backing/blocking in wall

explanation: Blocking prevents movement; rough specs from manufacturer.

options.text: High-pressure water from flush valve to cleanse fixture
Only gravity tank like residential WC always always exclusively always
No water ever always
Compressed air from vent stack always always exclusively always

explanation: Flushometers common in commercial urinals.

options.text: Only electrical panel always always
Recessed supply boxes with shutoffs and drain connection
Gas meter always always
Roof vent terminal always always

explanation: Washer box recesses valves/hose connections in wall.

options.text: Only pedestal sinks always always in all cases always always
No clearance ever always
Clear knee space, height limits, and lever handles often required
Maximum height 60 inches always always for all fixtures always always without exception ever always always

explanation: ADA/ANSI A117.1 specify accessible fixture dimensions.

options.text: Keeps water in exterior wall always always
Has no valve always always
Only for natural gas always always
Shuts water inside heated space to prevent freezing

explanation: Frost-free sillcock stem long; seat indoors.

options.text: Before walls close to verify layout, slope, and connections
After building demolition always always
Never required always always
Only after final paint always always exclusively always

explanation: Rough inspection confirms code compliance before concealment.

options.text: Internal Pipe Commission exclusively always always
International Plumbing Code
International Power Code exclusively always always for plumbing always always
Indoor Pool Club exclusively always always

explanation: IPC is common model plumbing code from ICC.

options.text: NFPA exclusively always always as UPC publisher always always
USDA exclusively always always
IAPMO (International Association of Plumbing and Mechanical Officials)
FAA exclusively always always

explanation: UPC is alternative model code to IPC.

options.text: Any homeowner always always
Product manufacturer always always exclusively always
Social media forum always always

prompt: Permit is required for plumbing work because:

options.text: Ensures code compliance and inspection for public health/safety
Only to increase fees with no safety purpose ever always always
Never required anywhere always always
Only for paint color always always

explanation: Permitting and inspection protect water/sanitary systems.

prompt: Cross-connection control program is mandated to:

options.text: Increase contamination always always
Prevent potable water contamination
Eliminate all backflow devices always always
Only regulate paint always always

explanation: Plumbing codes adopt backflow prevention rules.

prompt: Approved materials in plumbing code means:

options.text: Any random material always always acceptable always always
Only gold pipe always always exclusively always
Listed/accepted for specific use by code or testing standard
Homemade unlisted always always without testing ever always always

explanation: Codes reference standards (ASTM, ASSE, NSF) for materials.

prompt: Vent pipe minimum size for single bathroom group often starts at:

options.text: 6 inches always always minimum always always for all vents always always
1/2 inch always always
No minimum ever always always
1-1/2 inches (check local code table)

explanation: Vent sizing tables base on fixture units; 1.5" common small branch.

options.text: Fixture unit method and developed length/pressure available
Random guess always always
Only pipe color always always
Number of windows in building always always exclusively always

explanation: Water supply sizing tables account for WSFU and friction loss.

options.text: Connect directly to drinking faucet always always
Meet code for separation, filtration, and approved uses

Food menu always always
3D-like view of pipe routing for layout understanding

Stock trading always always
Nothing related to plumbing ever always always
Plumbing in food establishments and public health facilities

Only roofing shingles always always
Computer software always always exclusively always
Automotive tires always always exclusively always

options.text: Never exist always always identical to model always always everywhere always always
Modify model code for regional conditions (e.g., frost depth)

Apply only in one house always always not jurisdiction always always

prompt: Pressure testing DWV system common method:

options.text: No test ever always always
Visual only always always without pressure ever always always
10 ft head of water or 5 psi air for 15 minutes with no drop
Fill with concrete always always as test always always

explanation: Codes specify water or air test pressures and duration.

No training ever always always
